sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, after rotating, the green frame healed back to 144 pixels! The character is now at [26, 40]x[26, 40], which is still a 15x15 square. The character is upright with feet at the bottom.
Looking at the image, the character is upright with feet at the bottom, positioned to the left of the green frame. The character's right edge is at column 40, and the green frame's left edge is at column 41. So the character is adjacent to the green frame.
The green frame healed because the character is no longer overlapping with it (after rotation).
So the pattern is:
1. When the character overlaps with the green frame, the green frame breaks.
2. When the character doesn't overlap with the green frame, the green frame heals.
3. Rotating the character can change whether it overlaps with the green frame.
Now, the character is upright with feet at the bottom, adjacent to the green frame. The character's feet are at the bottom (rows 38-40 or so), and the green frame is at rows [29, 49].
Looking at the image, the character's feet (red) are at the bottom, and the green frame has red blocks on the left side. The character's feet are aligned with the lower red block inside the green frame.
Maybe the goal is to have the character's feet touch the red blocks inside the green frame. Let me try moving the character RIGHT to overlap with the green frame, with the feet at the bottom.
But wait, the character is at [26, 40]x[26, 40], and the green frame is at [29, 49]x[41, 49]. The character's right edge is at column 40, and the green frame's left edge is at column 41. So the character is adjacent to the green frame.
If I move RIGHT, the character would overlap with the green frame, causing it to break. But maybe the goal is to have the character's feet touch the red blocks inside the green frame.
Let me try moving RIGHT to see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Character upright at [26,40]x[26,40] with feet at bottom, adjacent to green frame at [29,49]x[41,49]. Green frame healed to 144 pixels after rotation (no overlap). Green frame breaks when character overlaps, heals when not overlapping.
Plan: Try moving RIGHT to overlap character with green frame while feet at bottom, to see if feet touch red blocks inside frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move RIGHT to overlap with green frame
action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"Step: {current_frame.step}, Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"White: rows [{min(rows)}, {max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}, {max(cols)}]")
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Green: rows [{min(rows)}, {max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}, {max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Green: rows [29, 49], cols [41, 49], pixels=126
